Therefore, this fits the 'partial-understanding' bucket.

Therefore, this fits the 'partial-understanding' bucket.

questions.question: Based on the text, why does the Taliban condemn the education of girls and not the education of boys?

However, it does not mention the inconsistency or hypocrisy that the Taliban wants female teachers and doctors but does not allow girls to go to school to become qualified for those jobs. The response is text-specific and shows understanding of the Taliban's reasoning, but it does not address the contradiction, so it fits the 'partial-understanding' bucket.

The response is text-specific and does not contradict the text, but it is missing the element of recognizing the double standard. Therefore, it fits the 'partial-understanding' bucket.

activities.text_title: Freedom Walkers: The Story of the Montgomery Bus

questions.question: In paragraph 21, Jo Ann Robinson said the verdict was a “bombshell.” What does she mean by this?

The response does not contradict the text and demonstrates a clear understanding of what the leaders were looking for.

questions.question: What is most closely a central idea of Vonnegut's letter to the students of Xavier High School?

responses.content: C. Pursuing art for art's sake makes a person's life better.

questions.question: Which sentence from the text most strongly supports the correct answer to the previous question?

responses.content: D. “Practice any art, music, singing, dancing, acting, drawing, painting, sculpting, poetry, fiction, essays, reportage, no matter how well or badly, not to get money and fame, but to experience becoming, to find out what's inside you, to make your soul grow.”

responses.content:
Have you ever wondered what it would be like to stand up for your rights when everyone else is afraid to? In my opinion the two stories are both different and similar at the same time. In Freedom walkers Claudette  didn’t get up from her seat and said it was her “constitutional right’ and then in Freedoms daughter she wanted a better school but the difference was the outcomes. The author focuses on the details and what happened in slight order like a roller coaster. The author more-ly payed attention to what happened on the bus in Freedom walkers, but in Freedoms Daughter And how they held an assembly for a strike on how they need a better school. They did this to not give up on their rights

The author develops the idea that Claudette actions cared about the law. I know this because in the story it states the Claudette did NOT move, and that she had the right to sit there. it proves my main idea because its true and that she learned about it

it shows that she cared, in 1951, April 23. Barbara and her classmates scheduled a meeting/assembly for a BETTER school OR none, I know this because from the passage it says "In the fall of 1950, Johns decided to take action on her own. She brought five other student leaders together for a clandestine student meeting in the bleachers of the school’s athletic field." this shows how determined they were. all of this shows on HOW she cared by working hard.

The two comparison in the story have slight same situations. freedoms walker decided to stay in her seat, because she had the right, and in freedoms daughter Barbara wanted a better school
